title: New, Orange-Lipped Monkey Discovered in Africa
author: Devin Reese
text: Subscribe to skip adsAdvertisementZoologyNew, Orange-Lipped Monkey Discovered in AfricaIt’s one of only a handful of new monkey species identified on the continent in the last 75 yearsDRBy Devin Reese1:01 PM CDT on July 15, 2026Share on FacebookShare on X (formerly Twitter)Share on RedditShare on EmailShare on BlueskyBy now, you’d think we’d have a handle on all the other primates sharing the Earth with us. After all, primates tend to be large-bodied, active, and noisy. And yet, a study led by Florida Atlantic University researchers just announced a new species from the east-central region of the Democratic Republic of the Congo: an orange-lipped colobus monkey dubbed Colobus congoensis.Subscribe to skip adsFeatured VideoPowered By 10 A Huge Number of Shark Deaths Is Causing a Gaping Hole in Ocean EcosystemsShareNextStayLIKWELI: Scientists identified the new species, Colobus congoensis, whose common name is the local vernacular name, “likweli,” using genetic, anatomical, and acoustic analyses. Photo by Daniel Rosengren.To be fair, inhabitants of the remote Congo basin were aware of the primate, with the Balanga community naming it “Likweli,” and the Mituku community calling it “Kasaba Nkoni” (or “branch shaker”—like other colobus, this one adeptly leaps between trees, causing branches to bow and sway). Still, local people had limited knowledge of the species because of its elusive habits. Already have an account?Log inJoin to continue readingYou've run out of free articles.
